name: Estación Plaza Constitución
geopoliticalNotes: Primary terminus for the Roca Line, the most heavily used commuter rail in Argentina. Beneficiary of BRI-funded rolling stock and infrastructure upgrades.
notes: Main gateway for the southern Buenos Aires metropolitan area.

name: Retiro Station Complex
geopoliticalNotes: Convergent point for the Mitre, Belgrano Norte, and San Martín lines. Central to the BRI-backed modernization of northern metropolitan lines.
notes: Adjacent to the Port of Buenos Aires, facilitating urban logistics.

name: Rosario Rail Export Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: The heart of Argentina's 'Agro-export' corridor. Terminus for the Belgrano Cargas (BRI project) bringing grain from the north.
notes: Critical for global soy and corn supply chains.

name: Córdoba Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Major industrial hub in central Argentina. Facilitates the movement of automotive parts and agricultural machinery to the ports.
notes: Key junction for the Central Argentine Railway network.

sources.name: CNRT (Comisión Nacional de Regulación del Transporte)

name: Paso de los Libres Border Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Critical rail border crossing with Brazil (Uruguaiana). Part of the Urquiza line and Mercosur logistics corridor.
notes: Handles significant trade in industrial components and foodstuffs between Argentina and Brazil.

name: Mendoza Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Gateway to the Andes. While the trans-Andean rail is currently limited, Mendoza remains the primary collection point for wine and mineral exports heading east.
notes: BRI investment plans include the reactivation of the San Martín line to Mendoza.

name: Bahía Blanca Port Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Deep-water port interface. Essential for liquid bulk (petroleum) and grain exports from southern Buenos Aires province.
notes: Major terminus for the Roca Line freight services.

sources.name: Consorcio de Gestión del Puerto de Bahía Blanca

name: Salta Rail Junction (Line C14)
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic link for the 'Lithium Triangle'. The C14 line connects northern Argentina to the Chilean port of Antofagasta.
notes: Part of the Belgrano Cargas network under renovation with Chinese financing.

name: Port of Santos Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Largest port in South America; primary exit point for Brazilian soy, corn, and sugar. Operated by Rumo and MRS Logística.
notes: Connects the state of São Paulo and the agricultural heartland to global markets.

name: Itaqui Rail Terminal (EFC)
geopoliticalNotes: Terminus of the Carajás Railroad (EFC). Critical for iron ore exports from the Carajás Mine, the world's largest iron ore deposit.
notes: Handles some of the world's heaviest and longest trains (up to 330 cars).

name: Tubarao Port Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Terminus of the Vitória-Minas Railway (EFVM). Essential for the Minas Gerais mining triangle logistics.
notes: Operates one of the few daily long-distance passenger services in Brazil.

name: Estação da Luz
geopoliticalNotes: Historical and functional heart of São Paulo's metropolitan rail network (CPTM and Metro).
notes: Critical for labor mobility in South America's largest city.

name: Central do Brasil
geopoliticalNotes: Main terminus for Rio's suburban rail network (SuperVia) and connection point for urban logistics.
notes: Iconic cultural and logistical landmark in Rio de Janeiro.

name: Araraquara Rail Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic inland hub and manufacturing site for CRRC (China Railway Rolling Stock Corporation), representing Chinese industrial presence in Brazil.
notes: Key junction for lines moving agricultural goods to the coast.

name: Rondonópolis Intermodal Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Critical agro-logistics hub in Mato Grosso. Connects the 'soy belt' to the Port of Santos via the Ferronorte line.
notes: Essential for global food security logistics.

name: Corumbá Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Primary rail border crossing with Bolivia. Part of the proposed Bioceanic Corridor to connect the Atlantic and Pacific.
notes: Mainly handles minerals and fuels between Brazil and Bolivia.

sources.name: ANUT - National Association of Transport Users

name: Xi'an International Port Station
geopoliticalNotes: Primary assembly center for China-Europe Railway Express (CRE). Handles the highest volume of BRI freight trains in China.
notes: Capacity expanded in 2024 to handle over 6,000 trains annually.

name: Zhengzhou North Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Largest freight marshaling yard in Asia. Key logistics crossroads for North-South and East-West corridors.
notes: Heart of China's 'Cross-shaped' rail network.

name: Alashankou Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Primary land port for BRI trade with Central Asia and Europe. Critical break-of-gauge point (standard to broad).
notes: Handles over 8,000 trains annually as of 2025.

name: Khorgas Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic gateway for the southern BRI corridor. Integral to the Khorgas-Eastern Gate SEZ.
notes: Fastest growing border crossing for CRE trains.

name: Manzhouli Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Largest land port on the China-Russia border. Handles timber, coal, and CRE freight.
notes: Key entry point for the Trans-Siberian rail connection.

name: Chengdu International Railway Port
geopoliticalNotes: Core hub for Western China's BRI outreach and the New Land-Sea Trade Corridor.
notes: Major node for electronics and automotive exports.

name: Erenhot Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Primary node for the China-Mongolia-Russia Economic Corridor.
notes: Main route for Mongolia's transit trade.

name: Chongqing Tuanjiecun Freight Station
geopoliticalNotes: Birthplace of the China-Europe Railway Express (YuXinOu line). Linking the Silk Road Economic Belt with the 21st Century Maritime Silk Road via the Yangtze.
notes: Critical intermodal hub.

name: Duisburg Port (Duisport)
geopoliticalNotes: World's largest inland port and the primary European gateway for the Belt and Road Initiative (BRI). Handles approximately 30% of all China-Europe rail trade.
notes: Freight volume cited as total port capacity including significant rail share.

name: Port of Hamburg Railway
geopoliticalNotes: Europe's largest rail port. Major sea-rail combined hub for both maritime and overland Silk Road routes.
notes: Handles over 200 freight trains daily.

name: Berlin Hauptbahnhof
geopoliticalNotes: Central transit node for East-West corridors. Increasing BRI-related air-rail cargo through surrounding logistics centers.
notes: Major passenger hub with growing freight significance on the East-West axis.

name: München Hauptbahnhof
geopoliticalNotes: Southern gateway for the Scan-Med Corridor. Critical for trans-Alpine rail freight and passenger transit to Italy and Austria.

name: Frankfurt (Main) Hauptbahnhof
geopoliticalNotes: Germany's busiest passenger hub and a vital link for connecting the Rhine-Main region. Extensive rail feeder services for Europe's largest air freight hub.

name: Leipzig/Halle Freight Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Major cargo hub integrating air and rail. Key for automotive logistics and BRI connections to China via the Via Regia corridor.
notes: Freight volume reflects the rail-integrated air freight hub capacity.

name: Nürnberg Hafen Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Central logistics hub in Bavaria with direct weekly rail connections to Chengdu, China. Vital node for South Germany's industrial exports.

name: Cologne-Gremberg Marshalling Yard
geopoliticalNotes: One of Germany's largest marshalling yards. Key for North-South and East-West transit in the industrial Rhineland.
notes: Critical node for DB Cargo operations in Western Germany.

name: Cairo Ramses Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary national rail hub of Egypt, connecting the Nile Delta to Upper Egypt. It is one of the busiest stations in Africa and the Middle East.
notes: Currently undergoing massive modernization of signaling and track systems.

name: Alexandria Misr Station
geopoliticalNotes: Major Mediterranean terminus and port-rail interface. Essential for moving maritime imports from Alexandria and Dekheila ports to the Egyptian interior.
notes: Northern terminus of the Cairo-Alexandria main line.

name: Ain Sokhna HSR Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: The eastern terminus of the 'Suez Canal on Rails' high-speed project. Integrated with the SCZone (Suez Canal Economic Zone), a major node for BRI maritime-rail integration.
notes: Starting point of the Green Line HSR connecting the Red Sea to the Mediterranean.

name: Bashtil Station (Said Misr)
geopoliticalNotes: A newly constructed mega-hub designed to relieve pressure on Ramses Station. It serves as the primary terminal for all trains traveling to and from Upper Egypt.
notes: Inaugurated in 2024; four times larger than Cairo's historic Ramses Station.

name: Port Said Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic link at the northern entrance of the Suez Canal. Key for the proposed Coastal HSR (Fourth Line) and BRI-related logistics in East Port Said Port.
notes: Essential for container transport from the Suez Canal zone.

name: Aswan Station
geopoliticalNotes: Southern terminus of the conventional Nile Valley line. Strategic gateway for future rail connectivity to Sudan (via Wadi Halfa).
notes: Critical for tourism and local connectivity in Upper Egypt.

name: Suez Station
geopoliticalNotes: Rail-port link at the southern entrance of the Suez Canal. Vital for the movement of fuel and chemicals from Suez refineries.
notes: Handles significant industrial freight traffic.

name: Marsa Matrouh HSR Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Western terminus of the High-Speed Rail Green Line. Strategic for developing Egypt's northwestern coast and potential future links to Libya.
notes: Part of the effort to decentralize the Egyptian population away from the Nile Valley.

name: Furi-Lebu (Labu) Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary passenger terminal for the capital and the western anchor of the Addis Ababa-Djibouti Railway, a flagship BRI project in Africa.
notes: Located on the southwestern outskirts of Addis Ababa.

name: Indode Freight Station
geopoliticalNotes: The main freight yard for the capital; serves as the primary terminal for unimodal cargo and bulk imports like fertilizers and grain.
notes: Houses the main rolling stock maintenance depot.

name: Modjo Dry Port & Station
geopoliticalNotes: Ethiopia's most critical inland logistics hub, handling ~78% of the nation's import-export trade.
notes: First dry port to have a direct rail spur in Ethiopia.

sources.name: Ethiopian Shipping and Logistics Services Enterprise (ESLSE)

name: Adama Station
geopoliticalNotes: Major commercial hub and transition point where the railway shifts from double-track to single-track.
notes: Key regional stop for the Madaraka Express equivalent (Ethio-Djibouti train).

name: Awash Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic junction where the Addis-Djibouti line meets the Awash-Hara Gebeya (AKH) line extending to northern Ethiopia.
notes: Gateway to the industrial zones of Kombolcha and Mekelle.

name: Dire Dawa Dry Port & Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary eastern hub; integrated with Ethiopia's first Free Trade Zone and major industrial parks.
notes: Critical for regional trade with the eastern Somali region and Djibouti.

sources.name: Ministry of Transport and Logistics, Ethiopia

name: Kombolcha Dry Port & Station
geopoliticalNotes: Northern hub on the AKH line; essential for transporting industrial goods from the Kombolcha Industrial Park to the coast.
notes: Key node in the Ethiopia-Djibouti-Northern Corridor.

name: Semera Logistics Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Regional administrative and logistics hub for the Afar region, serving as a critical road-rail link on the Djibouti corridor.
notes: Primarily handles livestock and salt transit.

name: Port Édouard-Herriot Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Principal inland hub for the Belt and Road Initiative (BRI) in France. Primary arrival point for direct trains from Wuhan, China.
notes: Handles significant volumes of containers via rail.

name: Delta 3 Dourges Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Major northern logistics hub and BRI node with direct links to Wuhan and Jinhua. Gateway to the UK and Benelux.
notes: Freight volume estimated based on TEU capacity (320,000 TEUs).

name: Port of Dunkirk Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: France's leading multimodal port for rail. Major hub for heavy industry and cross-Channel logistics.
notes: Handles 30% of the port's total land-based traffic.

name: HAROPA Port Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: France's largest container port. Vital for Atlantic trade and connecting the Seine axis to the European rail network.
notes: Freight volume based on 145,000 TEUs handled in 2024.

name: Marseille-Fos Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Key Mediterranean gateway and container hub. Strong connections to the Lyon-Paris national corridor.
notes: Freight volume based on 205,000 TEUs handled in 2024.

name: Valenton Intermodal Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Critical national hub connecting Paris to all major French corridors and the BRI routes via Germany and Belgium.

name: Saint-Charles International Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Vital hub for fresh produce and Mediterranean trade. Key link for the rail motorway between Spain and Northern Europe.
notes: Supports gauge-changing operations for traffic from Spain.

name: Modane Border Crossing Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Primary gateway for the Alpine crossing to Italy. Critical node for the trans-European transport network (TEN-T) Mediterranean corridor.
notes: Strategic importance remains high despite temporary line restrictions.

name: Barking Eurocentre (Ripple Lane)
geopoliticalNotes: Primary UK terminus for direct rail freight from China (Yiwu-London). Acts as a nodal yard for Thameside ports and international traffic via the Channel Tunnel.
notes: First direct train from China arrived here in January 2017.

sources.name: DB Cargo UK / China Railway Express

name: Port of Felixstowe Rail Terminals
geopoliticalNotes: UK's busiest container port. Handles approximately 25% of all port traffic by rail, operating 60 freight trains daily to inland hubs.
notes: Freight volume reflects the rail portion of port throughput (approx. 930,000 TEUs).

name: Port of Southampton Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: UK's second busiest container port and a major automotive hub. Highest rail modal share in the UK (approx. 33%).
notes: Handles around 42 trains daily with infrastructure for 775m-long trains.

name: Daventry International Rail Freight Terminal (DIRFT)
geopoliticalNotes: UK's largest inland intermodal terminal. Core node for national retail distribution and international traffic via the Channel Tunnel.
notes: Processes over 500,000 containers per year.

name: iPort Doncaster
geopoliticalNotes: Major northern logistics hub on the East Coast Main Line. Strategic link connecting northern industrial zones to deep-sea ports.
notes: Capacity for 12 trains per day with direct ECML access.

name: Dollands Moor Freight Yard
geopoliticalNotes: The primary international gateway for Channel Tunnel rail freight. Crucial for locomotive and security changes for all cross-border services.

name: Crewe Basford Hall
geopoliticalNotes: Key operational hub on the West Coast Main Line. Primary sorting and stabling point for freight moving between the South and North of the UK.
notes: Often referred to as the 'engine room' of the UK rail freight network.

name: St Pancras International
geopoliticalNotes: The UK terminus for High Speed 1 and Eurostar services. Critical node for international passenger connectivity to Europe.

name: Halim High-Speed Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Flagship BRI project (Whoosh). The first high-speed railway in Southeast Asia, connecting Jakarta and Bandung. Symbol of China-Indonesia strategic cooperation.
notes: Dedicated terminal for the 350 km/h high-speed service.

name: Tegalluar High-Speed Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The eastern terminus of the Whoosh HSR. Key node for future extensions toward Surabaya as part of the BRI-backed Trans-Java HSR network.
notes: Located in the Bandung metropolitan area, serving as a catalyst for local transit-oriented development.

name: Gambir Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Indonesia's primary executive-class passenger hub. Central to the conventional Java rail network.
notes: Located in the heart of the capital, near the National Monument.

name: Pasar Senen Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: High-volume passenger hub serving economy and business class long-distance routes across Java.
notes: One of the busiest stations in Indonesia during 'Mudik' (holiday exodus) periods.

name: Tanjung Priok Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Major port interface for Indonesia's busiest seaport. Critical for reducing road congestion in Jakarta by shifting container movement to rail.
notes: Connects directly to the national freight network.

name: Surabaya Gubeng Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Largest railway hub in East Java. Strategically positioned for the proposed extension of the BRI high-speed line from Bandung to Surabaya.
notes: Gateway for industrial and passenger traffic in the eastern part of Java.

name: Bandung Railway Station (Hall)
geopoliticalNotes: Key feeder hub for the Whoosh HSR. Integrates the conventional narrow-gauge network with the new standard-gauge BRI corridor.
notes: Critical for regional connectivity in West Java.

name: Semarang Tawang Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Central Java's primary logistics and passenger node. Linkage point for the North Coast (Pantura) rail corridor.
notes: Prone to tidal flooding, leading to strategic investments in flood mitigation for rail infrastructure.

name: Pt. Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Largest marshalling yard in Asia. Gateway between Northern and Eastern India. Critical node on the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) for coal and steel movement.
notes: Formerly known as Mughalsarai Junction.

name: Howrah Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Busiest railway station in India by passenger volume. Key terminal for Eastern and South Eastern Railways, serving as a primary link to Northeast India and Bangladesh.
notes: Anchor station for the Maitree Express to Dhaka.

name: New Delhi Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: National capital hub. Central node for the Vande Bharat high-speed network and primary military/administrative rail terminus.
notes: Major transformation under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus
geopoliticalNotes: UNESCO World Heritage site and the commercial rail backbone of India's financial capital. Directly linked to the Port of Mumbai and Western DFC.
notes: Hub for the Central Railway.

name: Secunderabad Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic pivot for South-Central India. Major hub for technology and defense logistics transport in the Deccan region.
notes: Headquarters of the South Central Railway.

name: Petrapole Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Most significant land border crossing for India-Bangladesh trade. Essential for the 'Maitri' (Friendship) connectivity projects.
notes: Handles high volumes of agricultural and textile trade.

name: Bilaspur Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Coal and mineral capital of Indian Railways. The highest freight-loading node in the country, powering thermal plants across Northern and Western India.
notes: South East Central Railway (SECR) headquarters.

name: Dadri Inland Container Depot
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic junction of the Eastern and Western Dedicated Freight Corridors. One of Asia's largest inland container ports, essential for North India's export-import trade.
notes: Critical intermodal node for JNPT port connectivity.

sources.name: Dedicated Freight Corridor Corporation of India (DFCCIL)

name: Aprin Dry Port
geopoliticalNotes: Iran's largest inland dry port and primary logistics hub for the Belt and Road Initiative (BRI). It serves as the main customs and distribution center for transit cargo between China and Europe.
notes: Expansion projects under BRI to increase capacity to 20 million tonnes annually.

name: Tehran Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The central node of Iran's rail network where the East-West (Silk Road) and North-South (INSTC) corridors intersect.
notes: Historical hub undergoing modernization for high-speed rail connectivity to Isfahan and Mashhad.

name: Mashhad Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic gateway for Central Asian freight entering Iran. Key node for the China-Kazakhstan-Turkmenistan-Iran rail corridor.
notes: Primary staging point for trains crossing the Sarakhs border.

name: Shahid Rajaee Port Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Iran's most critical maritime-rail interface. Handles the majority of Iran's containerized sea-to-rail transit, linking the Persian Gulf to Russia and Central Asia.
notes: Southern terminus of the North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).

name: Sarakhs Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Main border crossing between Iran and Turkmenistan. Features bogie exchange facilities for transition between standard and broad (1520mm) gauges.
notes: Critical bottleneck for BRI cargo originating from China via Central Asia.

name: Razi Rail Station
geopoliticalNotes: Only rail link between Iran and Turkey, serving as the gateway to Europe for BRI 'Southern Corridor' cargo.
notes: High elevation border crossing; capacity limited by the Lake Van ferry in Turkey.

name: Zahedan Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Connects Iran to the Pakistani rail network (Quetta-Zahedan line). Important for regional trade, though currently limited by different gauges (standard vs 1676mm broad).
notes: Potential future link to the CPEC (China-Pakistan Economic Corridor).

name: Incheh-Borun Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Secondary northern gateway on the Kazakhstan-Turkmenistan-Iran line. Provides a more direct route for bulk cargo from Russia and Kazakhstan to the Persian Gulf.
notes: Part of the Eastern route of the INSTC.

name: Tokyo Freight Terminal (Oi)
geopoliticalNotes: Japan's largest freight terminal and the primary base for the Tokaido Line. Critical for the 'Pacific Belt' economy, handling massive volumes of consumer goods.
notes: Terminus for 'Super Rail Cargo' high-speed freight trains. Gauge is 1067mm (narrow).

name: Suita Freight Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Primary consolidation point for the Kansai region. Vital node connecting Tokyo, Shikoku, and Kyushu freight corridors.
notes: Modern terminal replacing the former Umeda Freight Station.

name: Tokyo Station
geopoliticalNotes: The ultimate hub of Japan's rail network, connecting multiple Shinkansen lines. Critical for national economic integration and passenger mobility.
notes: Mixed gauge to support both Shinkansen (standard) and conventional (narrow) lines.

name: Shin-Osaka Station
geopoliticalNotes: Western terminus of the Tokaido Shinkansen and start of the Sanyo Shinkansen. The central passenger hub for the Kansai region.

name: Nagoya Freight Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Central logistics hub for the Chubu industrial heartland. Specializes in automotive parts and industrial machinery for major manufacturers like Toyota.

name: Kawasaki Freight Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Key terminal for the Keihin Industrial Zone, heavily integrated with the Port of Yokohama. Handles significant volumes of chemicals and raw materials.
notes: Handles approx. 700,000 TEUs annually.

name: Sapporo Freight Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Primary hub for Hokkaido's rail freight. Critical for transporting agricultural products to Honshu via the Seikan Tunnel.
notes: Vital for national food security (potato and onion transport).

name: Fukuoka Freight Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Main gateway for freight entering and leaving Kyushu. Handles agricultural products from the south and consumer goods from Honshu.

name: Khorgos Gateway Dry Port
geopoliticalNotes: A flagship BRI project, known as the 'Buckle' in the Belt and Road. It is the world's largest dry port, facilitating rapid transshipment between Chinese standard gauge and Eurasian broad gauge.
notes: Handles 80% of container trains between China and Europe. Capacity recently expanded to 800,000 TEUs.

name: Dostyk Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: The primary historical gateway of the Northern Corridor of the BRI. It connects the Chinese Lanxin railway to the Kazakhstan network.
notes: Critical for bulk commodities and minerals moving from Kazakhstan to China.

name: Aktau Sea North Terminal Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Key maritime-rail node for the Middle Corridor (Trans-Caspian International Transport Route). Connects China/Central Asia to the Caucasus and Europe via the Caspian Sea.
notes: Focusing on containerized transit to bypass Russian territory.

name: Nurly Zhol Station
geopoliticalNotes: A modern transportation hub symbolizing Kazakhstan's 'Nurly Zhol' infrastructure policy. Central administrative node for national rail operations.
notes: One of the largest and most advanced stations in Central Asia.

name: Almaty-1 Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary transit hub for southern Kazakhstan, connecting the Turk-Sib railway with the main East-West lines.
notes: Critical node for regional trade with Kyrgyzstan and Western China.

name: Kuryk Port Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: A new, specialized rail-ferry terminal on the Caspian Sea designed to facilitate the Middle Corridor transit, particularly for hazardous goods and heavy equipment.
notes: Complementary to Aktau, offering direct roll-on/roll-off rail ferry services.

name: Petropavlovsk Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: A unique station on the Trans-Siberian Railway located in Kazakhstan but operated by Russian Railways (RZD). A critical node for North-South and transit traffic between Russia and Central Asia.
notes: Handles high volumes of grain and coal exports.

name: Karaganda-Sortirovochnaya
geopoliticalNotes: The largest classification yard in Central Kazakhstan, serving the massive coal and steel industrial complex of the Karaganda region.
notes: Primary logistical node for domestic industrial freight.

name: Mombasa Terminus (Miritini)
geopoliticalNotes: The coastal gateway for the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR), linking the Port of Mombasa to the East African hinterland; a critical node in the BRI's presence in East Africa.
notes: Handles 40% of the Port of Mombasa's throughput.

sources.name: Kenya National Bureau of Statistics (KNBS) 2023 Report

name: Nairobi Terminus (Syokimau)
geopoliticalNotes: Central passenger hub for the Madaraka Express; connects the capital to the coast and serves as a model for modern rail transport in the region.
notes: Integrated with the Nairobi Commuter Rail system.

name: Nairobi Inland Container Depot (Embakasi)
geopoliticalNotes: The primary inland freight terminal in Kenya; centralizes containerized cargo for domestic distribution and regional transit.
notes: Directly linked to the SGR and the MGR for transshipment.

name: Naivasha ICD (Suswa)
geopoliticalNotes: Transshipment hub for cargo destined for Western Kenya and neighboring countries; reduces congestion on the Nairobi-Nakuru highway.
notes: Part of SGR Phase 2A; critical for the Northern Corridor's expansion.

sources.name: Standard Gauge Railway Phase 2A Project Data

name: Athi River Station
geopoliticalNotes: Strategically located near Nairobi's industrial zones; facilitates the transport of cement and construction materials.
notes: Major industrial node on the SGR line.

name: Mariakani Station
geopoliticalNotes: Logistics hub near Mombasa; serves as a weighbridge and marshalling point for up-country freight.
notes: Key technical stop for SGR freight trains.

name: Voi Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic junction for the branch line to Taveta/Tanzania border; connects the SGR to the MGR network for regional integration.
notes: Gateway to the Tsavo National Parks.

name: Kisumu Rail Port
geopoliticalNotes: Critical lake-rail interface on Lake Victoria; handles transit cargo to Uganda and Tanzania via wagon ferries.
notes: Currently served by the rehabilitated Meter Gauge Railway (MGR).

name: Busan New Port Station
geopoliticalNotes: South Korea's largest maritime gateway and the southern terminus of the Gyeongbu Line. Strategic starting point for the 'Iron Silk Road' vision connecting to Europe.
notes: Freight volume reflects the rail-integrated portion of Busan New Port's capacity.

name: Uiwang ICD (Obong Station)
geopoliticalNotes: Primary inland container depot for the Seoul Metropolitan Area. Handles over 50% of South Korea's total container rail traffic.
notes: Handles approx. 500,000 TEUs annually.

name: Seoul Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary passenger hub and KTX terminus of South Korea. Central node for national rail integration and political/economic connectivity.

name: Daejeon Station
geopoliticalNotes: Critical inland junction where the Gyeongbu (Seoul-Busan) and Honam (Seoul-Mokpo) lines meet. Key logistics and distribution center for central Korea.

name: Incheon Station (Port Terminal)
geopoliticalNotes: Major hub for trade with China and South Korea's second-largest container port. Vital for the Seoul-Incheon industrial corridor.
notes: Port volume cited as total capacity.

name: Taegum Station (Gwangyang Port)
geopoliticalNotes: Serves the Honam industrial region and the Gwangyang Steel Works. Key part of the national 'Two-Port' strategy to decentralize cargo from Busan.

name: Dorasan Station
geopoliticalNotes: The symbolic northernmost station in South Korea. Built as the gateway to connect the Trans-Korean Railway (TKR) to the Trans-Siberian and Trans-Chinese networks.
notes: Currently primarily symbolic; cross-border freight to Kaesong is suspended.

name: Poseung Station (Pyeongtaek Port)
geopoliticalNotes: Rapidly growing port and rail hub serving the major automotive and electronics clusters (Samsung, LG) in the southern Gyeonggi region.
notes: Critical node for industrial exports and component imports.

name: Terminal Intermodal Pantaco
geopoliticalNotes: Mexico's most important inland port and logistics hub, serving the capital's massive consumer market and industrial zones.
notes: Primary destination for containerized cargo from both Atlantic and Pacific ports.

sources.name: Secretaría de Infraestructura, Comunicaciones y Transportes (SICT)

name: Nuevo Laredo Rail Gateway
geopoliticalNotes: The busiest rail border crossing in North America. Critical for USMCA trade, especially automotive and industrial goods. Operated by CPKC.
notes: Handles over 50% of all rail freight between Mexico and the United States.

name: Monterrey Rail Yard (Ferromex/CPKC)
geopoliticalNotes: Industrial heart of Mexico and key transit node for 'nearshoring' manufacturing exports to the US market.
notes: Intersects major lines from the Pacific (Lázaro Cárdenas) and the North (Laredo/Juárez).

name: Port of Veracruz Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Mexico's primary Atlantic gateway. Essential for automotive exports and agricultural imports (grain).
notes: Serves as the main rail link to the Bajío industrial region.

sources.name: Administración del Sistema Portuario Nacional (ASIPONA) Veracruz

name: Lázaro Cárdenas Port Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Deep-water Pacific port specializing in containerized cargo from Asia. Direct rail link (CPKC) extends all the way to Chicago.
notes: A critical node in the Trans-Pacific supply chain.

name: Coatzacoalcos Interoceanic Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Atlantic terminus of the Tehuantepec Isthmus Interoceanic Corridor, a project designed as an alternative to the Panama Canal.
notes: Heavily promoted by the Mexican government for industrial development and global transit.

sources.name: Corredor Interoceánico del Istmo de Tehuantepec (CIIT)

name: Ciudad Juárez Rail Crossing
geopoliticalNotes: Major border hub connecting the Ferromex network to the Union Pacific (UP) and BNSF systems in El Paso, Texas.
notes: Focuses on maquiladora manufacturing and electronics logistics.

name: Cancún Airport Station (Tren Maya)
geopoliticalNotes: Flagship station of the Tren Maya project, designed to integrate the tourism economy of the Yucatán Peninsula.
notes: Includes freight capabilities expected to launch in late 2025/2026.

name: Port Klang Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Malaysia's primary maritime-rail hub. Currently being integrated into the ECRL (Phase 2) to create a land bridge between the Malacca Strait and the South China Sea.
notes: ECRL standard gauge expansion ongoing; expected full operation by 2028.

name: Padang Besar Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary rail gateway between Malaysia and Thailand. Critical node for the Pan-Asian Railway connecting Kunming to Singapore.
notes: Handles significant transshipment of rubber and electronics freight.

name: Johor Bahru Sentral
geopoliticalNotes: Southern terminus of the West Coast Line and primary link to Singapore. Integral to the RTS Link project which is 90% complete.
notes: Will serve as a major high-capacity transit node for the Singapore-Johor Special Economic Zone.

name: Kuantan Port Rail Terminal (ECRL)
geopoliticalNotes: A flagship BRI terminal on the East Coast Rail Link. Designed to facilitate cargo bypass of the Malacca Strait via the east-west land bridge.
notes: Targeted for full operational status in January 2027.

name: Kuala Lumpur Sentral
geopoliticalNotes: The central transport hub of Malaysia. Coordinates all major passenger rail lines including ETS, Komuter, and airport links.
notes: Critical node for urban and regional workforce mobility.

name: Gemas Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The strategic junction connecting the West Coast and East Coast rail lines. Crucial for domestic logistics and inter-state travel.
notes: Ongoing double-tracking projects to modernize the southern sector.

name: Butterworth Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Major northern port-rail interface serving the Penang Port and industrial zones in Kedah and Perak.
notes: Key terminal for the northern sector Komuter and ETS services.

name: Kota Bharu ECRL Station
geopoliticalNotes: Northern terminus of the ECRL, bringing standard gauge connectivity to the East Coast and potential future link to the Thai rail network.
notes: Infrastructure significantly complete; awaiting electrification and track laying.

name: Utrecht Centraal
geopoliticalNotes: The central node of the Dutch passenger rail network; almost all domestic intercity lines converge here, making it the most critical domestic transit hub.
notes: Houses Europe's largest bicycle parking facility.

name: Rotterdam Centraal
geopoliticalNotes: A major international gateway and high-speed rail hub; connects the Port of Rotterdam (the BRI's primary European maritime node) to the rest of Europe.
notes: Key stop for Eurostar and Thalys (now Eurostar) services.

name: Kijfhoek Shunting Yard
geopoliticalNotes: The largest shunting yard in the Netherlands and a vital link for the Port of Rotterdam; handles the assembly of freight trains for the Betuweroute and the European hinterland.
notes: Undergoing major automation upgrades by Siemens Mobility.

name: Venlo Station / Freight Hub
geopoliticalNotes: A critical border hub for rail freight moving to the German Ruhr region; serves as a dual-voltage point for locomotives crossing the border.
notes: Key node for the Brabant Route freight path.

name: Amsterdam Centraal
geopoliticalNotes: The primary international passenger terminal of the Netherlands and a symbol of European connectivity.
notes: Major renovations underway to increase capacity by 2030.

name: Schiphol Airport Station
geopoliticalNotes: A world-class example of air-rail integration; serves as a major node on the HSL-Zuid high-speed line.
notes: Crucial for reducing short-haul flights within Europe.

name: Moerdijk Port Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: A strategic intermodal hub connecting maritime, inland waterway, and rail traffic; specializes in chemical and containerized freight.
notes: Integrated with the Moerdijk Industrial Park.

name: Zevenaar Border Crossing
geopoliticalNotes: The primary exit point for the Betuweroute, the dedicated freight corridor linking the Port of Rotterdam to Germany; a cornerstone of the New Eurasian Land Bridge.
notes: Subject to major construction on the German side (Third Track).

name: Karachi Cantonment Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Southern terminus of the ML-1 (Main Line 1) under CPEC. Primary gateway for maritime freight from Karachi Port and Port Qasim to the rest of the country.
notes: Busiest freight hub in Pakistan Railways network.

name: Lahore Junction Station
geopoliticalNotes: Central passenger and freight hub. Headquarters of Pakistan Railways and a key node on the Eastern Route of CPEC. Historical rail link to India (Wagah).
notes: Primary passenger hub with connections to all major cities.

name: Rawalpindi Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic hub for the capital region (Islamabad/Rawalpindi). Critical node for the northern section of ML-1 and logistics for the twin cities.
notes: Handles significant military and civilian logistics due to proximity to GHQ.

name: Quetta Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Terminus for ML-3. Critical link for trade with Iran (via Taftan) and potentially Afghanistan. Strategic for mineral transport from Balochistan.
notes: High elevation station with challenging terrain connectivity.

name: Peshawar Cantonment Station
geopoliticalNotes: Northern terminus of ML-1. Gateway for transit trade with Afghanistan and a key component of the proposed Trans-Afghan Railway linking Uzbekistan to the Arabian Sea.
notes: Primary hub for Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province.

sources.name: Ministry of Transport - CPEC ML-1 Updates

name: Multan Cantonment Station
geopoliticalNotes: Major agricultural and industrial hub in South Punjab. Significant junction on ML-1 and a transit point for the Central Route of CPEC.
notes: Key for fertilizer and cotton freight transport.

name: Gwadar Port Rail Terminal (Planned)
geopoliticalNotes: The southern terminus of the CPEC corridor. Designed to link the Gwadar deep-sea port to Kashgar, China, providing an alternative to the Malacca Strait.
notes: Infrastructure currently in land acquisition and design phase; part of ML-4.

name: Rohri Junction Station
geopoliticalNotes: Critical railway junction where the line from Quetta (ML-3) meets the main Karachi-Peshawar line (ML-1). Essential for connectivity between Sindh and Balochistan.
notes: Strategic transit point for mineral and agricultural freight.

name: Małaszewicze Logistics Park
geopoliticalNotes: The 'Gateway to Europe' for the New Silk Road. Handles over 80% of rail container traffic between China and the EU. Critical transshipment point between 1520mm and 1435mm gauges.
notes: Handles a surge in traffic (up 77% in H1 2024) due to Red Sea maritime disruptions.

name: Port of Gdańsk Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: The largest container terminal on the Baltic Sea. Primary node for the Baltic-Adriatic corridor, connecting Northern Europe to the Balkans and the Middle East.
notes: Rail handles 35% of the port's hinterland cargo.

name: Łódź Olechów Intermodal Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: A central intermodal hub in Poland and a primary destination for BRI trains from Chengdu. Serves as a distribution center for Western Europe.
notes: Capacity expanding to 530,000 TEU per year by 2027.

name: Przemyśl Główny
geopoliticalNotes: The most critical rail link between the EU and Ukraine. Handles massive volumes of grain exports, humanitarian aid, and refugee transit since 2022.
notes: Features both standard and 1520mm broad gauge tracks reaching into the city center.

name: Euroterminal Sławków
geopoliticalNotes: Westernmost point of the LHS broad gauge line (1520mm), allowing direct rail freight from Asia and Ukraine deep into the EU without axle changes.
notes: Strategically positioned for the future reconstruction of Ukraine.

name: Warszawa Centralna
geopoliticalNotes: The primary passenger and administrative hub of Poland. Central node for the E20 East-West and North-South rail corridors.
notes: Undergoing extensive modernization of the cross-city line to increase throughput.

name: Poznań Główny
geopoliticalNotes: Key node on the Berlin-Warsaw-Moscow corridor. A major transit point for BRI freight moving from Małaszewicze toward Germany and the Benelux countries.
notes: One of Poland's busiest passenger stations by foot traffic.

name: Port of Gdynia Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Poland's second-largest port interface, specializing in grain and general cargo. Key military port for NATO logistics on the Baltic Sea.
notes: Features a specialized military rail ramp for NATO equipment deployment.

name: Bekasovo-Sortirovochnoe
geopoliticalNotes: One of the largest marshaling yards in Europe. Central node for the Moscow Railway, distributing 60% of Russia's rail imports from both Europe and the BRI Northern Corridor.
notes: Key technical hub for automated classification.

name: Zabaikalsk Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Russia's most important land border crossing with China. Primary gateway for the BRI Northern Corridor and Trans-Siberian export traffic.
notes: Handles significant break-of-gauge operations.

name: Sverdlovsk-Sortirovochny
geopoliticalNotes: Heart of the Trans-Siberian Railway. Critical bridge between European Russia and the Asian territories, serving as a major consolidation point for BRI freight.
notes: Logistics capital of the Urals.

name: Vladivostok Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Eastern terminus of the Trans-Siberian Railway. Strategic gateway for Russian exports to the Asia-Pacific region and a key node for the Maritime Silk Road integration.
notes: Includes the Vladivostok Commercial Sea Port rail interface.

name: Novosibirsk-Glavny
geopoliticalNotes: Administrative and logistical capital of Siberia. Major junction where the Trans-Siberian meets the Turksib railway and BRI routes from Kazakhstan.
notes: Busiest passenger station in Siberia.

name: Naushki Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Primary gateway for the China-Mongolia-Russia Economic Corridor (CMREC). Essential for transit trade through Ulaanbaatar.
notes: Currently undergoing capacity expansion to 15M tonnes.

name: Ust-Luga Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Russia's largest Baltic port interface. Strategically designed to bypass Baltic states for energy and container exports. Anchor of the Maritime Silk Road's European terminus.
notes: Handles massive coal and oil volumes.

name: Chelyabinsk-Glavny
geopoliticalNotes: Major metallurgical and industrial hub. Key junction on the southern Trans-Siberian branch and a vital node for the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
notes: High strategic value for heavy industry logistics.

name: Krung Thep Aphiwat Central Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Southeast Asia's largest railway station. The primary terminus for the China-Thailand High-Speed Railway, connecting Bangkok to Kunming via Vientiane.
notes: Replaced Hua Lamphong as the national rail hub; central to the Pan-Asian Railway network.

name: Nong Khai Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The 'Golden Link' of the BRI in Thailand. Direct connection to the China-Laos Railway, facilitating rapid land trade between ASEAN and China.
notes: Handles significant transshipment from standard gauge (Lao/China) to meter gauge (Thai).

name: Laem Chabang Port Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Thailand's main deep-sea port and a key component of the Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC). Integrates with the BRI's 21st Century Maritime Silk Road.
notes: Includes the Single Rail Transfer Operator (SRTO) facility for efficient container logistics.

name: Hat Yai Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Southern Thailand's most important hub. Critical node in the Singapore-Kunming Rail Link (SKRL) and terminus for the ASEAN Express cargo service.
notes: Connects the Thai network to the Malaysian (KTMB) system at Padang Besar.

sources.name: State Railway of Thailand Southern Division

name: Nakhon Ratchasima (Khorat) Station
geopoliticalNotes: The 'Gateway to the Northeast'. Terminus for Phase 1 of the China-Thailand High-Speed Railway. Major junction for lines to Nong Khai and Ubon Ratchathani.
notes: Undergoing massive redevelopment to accommodate high-speed rail operations.

sources.name: Bangkok Post - EEC and Rail Updates

name: Kaeng Khoi Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic junction where the Northern, Northeastern, and Eastern Seaboard lines converge. Critical for bypass freight routing.
notes: Heavy industrial rail traffic due to nearby cement and manufacturing plants.

name: Chachoengsao Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Major junction for the Eastern Seaboard and the line to Aranyaprathet (Cambodia border). Key node in the EEC logistics network.
notes: Vital for commuters and freight moving between Bangkok and the industrial east.

name: Padang Besar Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary rail border crossing between Thailand and Malaysia. Facilitates the ASEAN Express cargo service from Chongqing to Kuala Lumpur.
notes: Co-located customs and immigration for both countries within the station complex.

name: Istanbul Halkalı Station
geopoliticalNotes: Primary gateway to Europe and the western terminus for the Middle Corridor (Iron Silk Road). Connects Turkey to the EU rail network via Bulgaria.
notes: Handles both high-speed passenger and trans-continental freight traffic.

name: Ankara YHT Station
geopoliticalNotes: Central hub for Turkey's rapidly expanding High-Speed Rail (YHT) network and a key stop for Middle Corridor freight trains moving from China to Europe.
notes: Dedicated high-speed terminal adjacent to the historic Ankara station.

name: Köseköy Logistics Center
geopoliticalNotes: The largest logistics center in the Marmara region; critical for industrial exports and a primary consolidation point for BRI freight.
notes: Serves the Kocaeli industrial heartland.

name: Kars Logistics Center
geopoliticalNotes: The Turkish gateway for the Baku-Tbilisi-Kars (BTK) railway, bypassing Russia to link China and Central Asia directly to Turkey.
notes: Essential for the Trans-Caspian International Transport Route.

name: Mersin Port Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Major Mediterranean rail-port interface. Critical for the proposed 'Development Road' link from Iraq to Turkey and Europe.
notes: One of the most active freight hubs in the Middle East/Mediterranean.

name: Kapıkule Border Station
geopoliticalNotes: Busiest rail border crossing between Turkey and the European Union. Part of the Iron Silk Road's final leg into Europe.
notes: Undergoing high-speed upgrades to connect with Istanbul.

name: Izmir Alsancak Station
geopoliticalNotes: Key Aegean port interface for agricultural and industrial exports from Western Anatolia.
notes: Integrates with the Izban suburban rail system.

name: İskenderun Port Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic port serving the heavy industry and steel production in Hatay. Potential southern node for future Middle East rail links.
notes: Handles significant mineral and metal freight.

name: Kyiv-Pasazhyrskyi
geopoliticalNotes: Central hub of the Ukrainian rail network; critical for both domestic troop movements and international passenger/freight corridors linking the EU to the East.
notes: Primary station for the 'Solidarity Lanes' initiative.

name: Lviv-Holovnyi
geopoliticalNotes: Main western gateway to the EU; undergoing conversion to standard gauge to integrate with European TEN-T corridors.
notes: Critical for logistics and humanitarian aid since 2022.

name: Chop Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Triple-border transshipment hub where broad and standard gauge tracks meet; vital for BRI traffic from China to Budapest and Vienna.
notes: Major bogie exchange and container transshipment facility.

name: Odesa-Holovna / Port Interface
geopoliticalNotes: Main maritime-rail gateway for the 'Middle Corridor'; essential for grain exports and global food security.
notes: Includes Chornomorsk and Pivdennyi port rail extensions.

name: Yahodyn Freight Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Busiest rail crossing to Poland for grain and iron ore; primary northern corridor to Warsaw and Berlin.
notes: Key terminal for the Izov-Hrubieszow line.

name: Kovel Junction
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic northern junction connecting the central regions to the Polish border crossings and the Baltic states.
notes: Hub for timber and mineral exports.

name: Izov Freight Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Primary exit point for iron ore exports to Poland via the Broad Gauge Metallurgy Line (LHS).
notes: Crucial for the Ukrainian steel industry's exports to the EU.

name: Vadul-Siret Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Main rail gateway to Romania and the Port of Constanța; key for bypassing the Black Sea blockade.
notes: Handles significant grain and petroleum products.

name: Clearing Yard
geopoliticalNotes: The most critical rail interchange in North America. Connects all six Class I railroads. Handling 25% of all US rail freight traffic.
notes: Operated by the Belt Railway of Chicago.

name: Argentine Yard
geopoliticalNotes: Top rail hub by tonnage in the US. Critical node for agricultural exports and Powder River Basin coal distribution.
notes: Primary BNSF classification yard.

name: Laredo Rail Port of Entry
geopoliticalNotes: Number one rail port of entry on the US-Mexico border. Essential for USMCA automotive and industrial supply chains.
notes: Includes the Sanchez Yard on the Mexican side.

name: Hobart Yard
geopoliticalNotes: The busiest intermodal rail terminal in the US, servicing the Port of Los Angeles and Port of Long Beach.
notes: Main gateway for trans-Pacific container trade into the US interior.

name: Englewood Yard
geopoliticalNotes: Nexus for the US petrochemical industry. Critical for the transport of plastics, chemicals, and energy products.
notes: Major Union Pacific hub.

name: Inman Yard
geopoliticalNotes: Logistics backbone of the Southeastern US. Vital for consumer goods and automotive distribution in the region.
notes: Operated by Norfolk Southern.

name: Madison Yard
geopoliticalNotes: Second largest rail hub in the US by interchange volume. Strategic bridge over the Mississippi River connecting Eastern and Western lines.
notes: Managed by the Terminal Railroad Association of St. Louis.

sources.name: Terminal Railroad Association of St. Louis

name: Pennsylvania Station
geopoliticalNotes: Busiest passenger station in North America. Anchor of the Northeast Corridor, vital for regional economic stability.
notes: Owned by Amtrak; used by LIRR and NJ Transit.

name: Đồng Đăng International Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary rail gateway between Vietnam and China. Features dual-gauge tracks (1,000mm and 1,435mm) enabling direct transit of Chinese standard-gauge trains into the Hanoi region.
notes: Key entry point for the China-Indochina Peninsula Economic Corridor.

name: Yên Viên Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The most important freight hub in Northern Vietnam. Serves as the consolidation point for international container trains bound for Central Asia and Europe via China.
notes: Handles the 'Hanoi-Chengdu' and 'Hanoi-Liege' intermodal services.

name: Sóng Thần Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The largest and most critical freight terminal in Southern Vietnam. Connects the industrial heartland of Binh Duong and HCMC to the national network.
notes: Planned southern terminus for the future North-South High-Speed Rail (standard gauge).

name: Lào Cai Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Major border crossing connecting to Hekou, China (Yunnan province). Critical for the export of minerals and agricultural products to Southwest China.
notes: Standard-gauge upgrade project (Lao Cai-Hanoi-Haiphong) is a top priority for BRI integration.

name: Hanoi Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The administrative and passenger heart of the Vietnamese rail network. Connecting point for the North-South (Reunification Express) and Northern lines.
notes: Subject to major urban redevelopment and future integration with Hanoi Metro.

name: Haiphong Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary rail-to-sea gateway for Northern Vietnam. Serves as the maritime terminus for the Lao Cai-Hanoi-Haiphong economic corridor.
notes: Critical for fertilizer, sulfur, and industrial container transit.

name: Da Nang Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: The main rail hub for Central Vietnam, facilitating trade and tourism along the North-South axis.
notes: Strategically located midway between Hanoi and HCMC.

name: Vinh Railway Station
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic hub in North-Central Vietnam. Potential future connection point for the planned Vung Ang–Vientiane railway linking Vietnam and Laos.
notes: Handles significant transit traffic between the north and south.

name: City Deep Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: The largest inland 'dry port' in Africa. Critical for South Africa's container logistics, linking the industrial heartland of Gauteng to the Port of Durban.
notes: Handles major automotive and general freight for the SADC region.

sources.name: Mordor Intelligence - South Africa Freight Rail Market

name: Durban Port Rail Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Busiest port in sub-Saharan Africa. The rail-port interface is the southern anchor of the Container Corridor, essential for international trade.
notes: Handles ~70% of South Africa's total container volume.

name: Richards Bay Coal Terminal (RBCT) Rail Hub
geopoliticalNotes: Terminus of the heavy-haul Coal Line. Critical for South Africa's mineral exports, particularly coal and magnetite, to global markets.
notes: One of the world's leading coal export terminals by volume.

sources.name: Transnet Freight Rail - Coal Line Statistics

name: Saldanha Bay Iron Ore Terminal
geopoliticalNotes: Terminus of the 861 km Ore Corridor from Sishen. Strategic for the export of high-grade iron ore, primarily to Asian markets.
notes: Dedicated heavy-haul line with some of the longest trains in the world.

sources.name: Transnet Freight Rail - Ore Line Operations

name: Sentrarand Marshalling Yard
geopoliticalNotes: The primary rail distribution hub for South Africa. Most general freight trains are consolidated here for dispatch to various national and regional corridors.
notes: Critical 'nerve center' for the national freight network.

sources.name: Transnet Freight Rail - General Freight Business

name: Pretoria Gautrain Station
geopoliticalNotes: Africa's first high-speed rapid rail hub. Connects the administrative capital to Johannesburg and the international airport, serving the political and business elite.
notes: Operates on standard gauge, distinct from the national narrow-gauge network.

name: Cape Town Station
geopoliticalNotes: Strategic hub for the Western Cape. Key for agricultural exports and the primary terminal for the Metrorail Western Cape commuter network.
notes: Gateway for the 'Garden Route' and southern Atlantic trade.

name: Musina Border Station
geopoliticalNotes: The primary rail border crossing between South Africa and Zimbabwe (Beitbridge). Essential link for the North-South Corridor connecting SA to Zambia and DRC.
notes: Critical for regional SADC trade integration.

sources.name: SADC Regional Infrastructure Development Master Plan
